title: Cannot pull image: invalid signature when validating ASN.1 encoded signature
body:
### Issue Description

I've been running distrobox containers on Fedora Silverblue 38 for a while and recently something is broken. I get the following error when I try to pull the image:

```
$ podman pull ghcr.io/tunix/pengutool
Trying to pull ghcr.io/tunix/pengutool:latest...
Error: Source image rejected: cryptographic signature verification failed: invalid signature when validating ASN.1 encoded signature
```

Googling this error, unfortunetely, reveals nothing.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Pulling the image is sufficient to reprocude error on my computer: `podman pull ghcr.io/tunix/pengutool`

### Describe the results you received

```

### Describe the results you expected

I expect that the download goes fine and that the container is successfully pulled.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

It happens everytime I pull this particular image.

comments.body:
It works for me:


```
podman pull ghcr.io/tunix/pengutool 
Trying to pull ghcr.io/tunix/pengutool:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 509a334a6f49 done  
Copying blob 8e78d5ae102e done  
Copying blob 1c02a04b932a done  
Copying config 5242ba544a done  
Writing manifest to image destination
WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64) does not match the expected platform (linux/arm64)
5242ba544ae62ba4476abc482846b861f1b721ac48b5c0e359205fa8b97665bf

> It works for me:
> 
> ```
> podman pull ghcr.io/tunix/pengutool 
> Trying to pull ghcr.io/tunix/pengutool:latest...
> Getting image source signatures
> Copying blob 509a334a6f49 done  
> Copying blob 8e78d5ae102e done  
> Copying blob 1c02a04b932a done  
> Copying config 5242ba544a done  
> Writing manifest to image destination
> WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64) does not match the expected platform (linux/arm64)
> 5242ba544ae62ba4476abc482846b861f1b721ac48b5c0e359205fa8b97665bf
> ```

Yes, that's the problem. I'm trying to figure out what might be wrong in my setup. I've tried with the root user but that didn't work either.

Looks like podman keeps some repository metadata in `/etc/containers` which causes the problem. This is unexpected to me since these are user data, not some system data that's worth to keep in there. One of the files were `/etc/containers/policy.json`. I edited the file and removed the related one. There was also a dedicated file at `/etc/containers/registries.d/penguix.yaml` -- i deleted this one. Now, I'm able to pull my image.
